rules.label: Prohibition of subliminal/manipulative/deceptive AI causing significant harm
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of an AI system that deploys subliminal, purposefully manipulative or deceptive techniques materially distorting behaviour and causing significant harm is prohibited.
rules.scope: AI systems using subliminal or manipulative/deceptive techniques

rules.label: Prohibition of exploiting vulnerabilities (age, disability, social/economic situation)
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of an AI system exploiting vulnerabilities due to age, disability or social/economic situation to materially distort behaviour causing significant harm is prohibited.
rules.scope: AI systems exploiting vulnerable persons or groups

rules.label: Prohibition of social scoring leading to detrimental treatment
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of AI systems for social scoring of natural persons leading to unjustified or disproportionate detrimental treatment is prohibited.
rules.scope: AI systems evaluating/classifying persons based on social behaviour or characteristics

rules.label: Prohibition of crime risk prediction based solely on profiling
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of an AI system to assess/predict the risk of a natural person committing a criminal offence based solely on profiling or personality traits is prohibited.
rules.scope: AI systems for criminal risk assessment of natural persons
exceptions: Does not apply to AI systems supporting human assessment based on objective and verifiable facts directly linked to a criminal activity

rules.label: Prohibition of facial recognition database creation via untargeted scraping
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of AI systems that create or expand facial recognition databases through untargeted scraping of facial images from the internet or CCTV footage is prohibited.
rules.scope: AI systems building facial recognition databases by scraping

rules.label: Prohibition of emotion inference in workplace and education
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of AI systems to infer emotions of natural persons in the workplace and education institutions is prohibited.
rules.scope: Emotion recognition AI systems in workplace or education institutions
exceptions: Use intended for medical or safety reasons is permitted

rules.label: Prohibition of biometric categorisation inferring sensitive attributes
rules.obligation: The placing on the market, putting into service or use of biometric categorisation systems that categorise natural persons based on biometric data to deduce race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life or sexual orientation is prohibited.
rules.scope: Biometric categorisation systems inferring sensitive characteristics
exceptions: Does not cover labelling or filtering of lawfully acquired biometric datasets or categorising of biometric data in the area of law enforcement

rules.label: Prohibition of real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces for law enforcement
rules.obligation: The use of real-time remote biometric identification systems in publicly accessible spaces for law enforcement is prohibited unless strictly necessary for one of the enumerated objectives (targeted search for victims, prevention of substantial imminent threat/terrorist attack, localisation of suspects of serious offences).
rules.scope: Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces for law enforcement
exceptions: Targeted search for specific victims of abduction, trafficking, sexual exploitation or missing persons
Prevention of specific, substantial and imminent threat to life/physical safety or terrorist attack
Localisation/identification of suspects of Annex II offences punishable by at least four years

rules.label: Conditions and safeguards for permitted real-time biometric identification use
rules.obligation: Use must be limited to confirming the identity of a specifically targeted individual, take into account the nature of the situation and consequences, comply with necessary and proportionate safeguards, and only be authorised after a fundamental rights impact assessment (Article 27) and registration in the EU database (Article 49), with urgent cases allowing deferred registration.
rules.scope: Permitted real-time remote biometric identification for law enforcement
exceptions: In duly justified cases of urgency, registration may be completed without undue delay after commencement

rules.label: Prior judicial/administrative authorisation for real-time biometric identification
rules.obligation: Each use must be subject to prior authorisation by a judicial or independent administrative authority upon reasoned request; in urgent cases use may commence without authorisation provided it is requested within 24 hours, and if rejected use must stop immediately and all data discarded.
rules.scope: Authorisation procedure for real-time remote biometric identification for law enforcement
exceptions: Duly justified urgency permits commencement before authorisation if requested within 24 hours

rules.label: High-risk classification: safety component under Annex I with third-party conformity assessment
rules.obligation: An AI system intended to be used as a safety component of a product (or that is itself a product) covered by Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I, where that product is required to undergo a third-party conformity assessment, shall be considered high-risk.
rules.scope: AI systems that are safety components or products under Annex I Union harmonisation legislation

rules.label: High-risk classification: Annex III systems
rules.obligation: AI systems referred to in Annex III shall be considered to be high-risk, in addition to those under paragraph 1.
rules.scope: AI systems in Annex III use-case areas
exceptions: Article 6(3) derogation where no significant risk of harm

rules.label: Derogation: Annex III system not high-risk where no significant risk
rules.obligation: By derogation from paragraph 2, an Annex III AI system shall not be considered high-risk where it does not pose a significant risk of harm and meets one of the listed conditions (narrow procedural task, improving a completed human activity, detecting deviations without replacing human assessment, or performing a preparatory task).
rules.scope: Annex III AI systems that do not materially influence decision-making outcomes
exceptions: Always high-risk where the system performs profiling of natural persons

rules.label: Profiling override: always high-risk
rules.obligation: Notwithstanding the derogation, an Annex III AI system shall always be considered high-risk where it performs profiling of natural persons.
rules.scope: Annex III AI systems performing profiling of natural persons

rules.label: Provider documentation of non-high-risk assessment
rules.obligation: A provider who considers that an Annex III AI system is not high-risk shall document its assessment before placing on the market or putting into service, shall comply with the registration obligation in Article 49(2), and shall provide the documentation to national competent authorities upon request.
rules.scope: Providers claiming the Article 6(3) derogation for Annex III systems

rules.label: Commission guidelines on implementation
rules.obligation: The Commission shall, after consulting the Board and no later than 2 February 2026, provide guidelines specifying the practical implementation of this Article together with a comprehensive list of practical examples of high-risk and non-high-risk use cases.
rules.scope: European Commission

rules.label: Commission delegated acts to add/modify derogation conditions
rules.obligation: The Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, by adding or modifying conditions where there is concrete and reliable evidence of Annex III systems that do not pose a significant risk of harm.
rules.scope: European Commission

rules.label: Commission delegated acts to delete derogation conditions
rules.obligation: The Commission shall adopt delegated acts to amend paragraph 3, second subparagraph, by deleting any condition where there is concrete and reliable evidence necessary to maintain the level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights.
rules.scope: European Commission

rules.label: Constraints on amendments to derogation conditions
rules.obligation: Any amendment to the conditions under paragraphs 6 and 7 shall not decrease the overall level of protection of health, safety and fundamental rights, shall ensure consistency with delegated acts under Article 7(1), and shall take account of market and technological developments.
rules.scope: European Commission amendments

rules.label: Union harmonisation legislation based on New Legislative Framework (Section A)
rules.obligation: An AI system intended as a safety component of, or itself a product covered by, the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I Section A (machinery, toys, recreational craft, lifts, ATEX equipment, radio equipment, pressure equipment, cableway installations, PPE, gas appliances, medical devices, in vitro diagnostic medical devices) is treated as high-risk under Article 6(1) when conformity assessment is required.
rules.scope: AI systems that are products or safety components of products covered by NLF-based Union harmonisation legislation

rules.label: Other Union harmonisation legislation (Section B)
rules.obligation: An AI system intended as a safety component of, or itself a product covered by, the Union harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I Section B (civil aviation security, two/three-wheel vehicles, agricultural/forestry vehicles, marine equipment, rail interoperability, motor vehicles type-approval, general vehicle safety, civil aviation/EASA for unmanned aircraft) is treated as high-risk under Article 6(1) when conformity assessment is required.
rules.scope: AI systems that are products or safety components of products covered by other Union harmonisation legislation listed in Section B

rules.label: Remote biometric identification systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used as remote biometric identification systems are classified as high-risk under Annex III, in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law.
rules.scope: Biometric identification systems
exceptions: Biometric verification systems whose sole purpose is to confirm that a specific natural person is who they claim to be

rules.label: Biometric categorisation by sensitive attributes is high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems intended to be used for biometric categorisation according to sensitive or protected attributes based on inference are high-risk, in so far as permitted under Union or national law.
rules.scope: Biometric categorisation systems

rules.label: Emotion recognition systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems intended to be used for emotion recognition are high-risk, in so far as permitted under Union or national law.
rules.scope: Emotion recognition systems

rules.label: Critical infrastructure safety components are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, or supply of water, gas, heating or electricity are high-risk.
rules.scope: Critical infrastructure safety components

rules.label: Education and vocational training AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used to determine access/admission, assign persons to institutions, evaluate learning outcomes, assess appropriate level of education, or monitor prohibited behaviour during tests in educational and vocational training institutions are high-risk.
rules.scope: Education and vocational training

rules.label: Employment and worker management AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used for recruitment/selection, or to make decisions affecting terms of work-related relationships, promotion/termination, task allocation based on traits, or monitoring/evaluating performance are high-risk.
rules.scope: Employment, workers' management and access to self-employment

rules.label: Creditworthiness evaluation AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems intended to evaluate creditworthiness of natural persons or establish their credit score are high-risk.
rules.scope: Access to essential private services - credit
exceptions: AI systems used for the purpose of detecting financial fraud

rules.label: Law enforcement AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used by or on behalf of law enforcement for victim risk assessment, polygraphs, evidence reliability evaluation, offending/re-offending risk assessment, or profiling are high-risk, in so far as permitted under Union or national law.
rules.scope: Law enforcement

rules.label: Migration, asylum and border control AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used by competent public authorities for polygraphs, risk assessment of persons entering the territory, examination of asylum/visa/residence applications, or detecting/identifying persons in migration/border contexts are high-risk, in so far as permitted under Union or national law.
rules.scope: Migration, asylum and border control management

rules.label: Essential public/private services and benefits AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used by public authorities to evaluate eligibility for essential public assistance benefits, to evaluate creditworthiness, for risk assessment and pricing of life/health insurance, or to evaluate/dispatch emergency calls and first response services are high-risk.
rules.scope: Access to and enjoyment of essential private and public services and benefits
exceptions: AI systems used for the purpose of detecting financial fraud (for creditworthiness)

rules.label: Administration of justice and democratic processes AI systems are high-risk
rules.obligation: AI systems used to assist judicial authorities in researching/interpreting facts and applying law (or in ADR), or to influence the outcome of elections/referenda or voting behaviour, are high-risk.
rules.scope: Administration of justice and democratic processes
exceptions: AI systems to whose output natural persons are not directly exposed, such as tools to organise, optimise or structure political campaigns from an administrative or logistical point of view

scenarios.label: HR screening tool
scenarios.description: AI ranks job applicants by predicted suitability for a role

scenarios.label: Creditworthiness assessment
scenarios.description: AI assesses creditworthiness for consumer loan applications

scenarios.label: Facial recognition (public space)
scenarios.description: Real-time biometric identification by law enforcement in a publicly accessible shopping centre

scenarios.label: Narrow procedural task (Art 6(3))
scenarios.description: HR system claiming Article 6(3) exception — performs a narrow preparatory task only, with human override

scenarios.label: Customer service chatbot
scenarios.description: General-purpose customer support assistant, no high-risk use case
